meta.purpose: Proof-of-concept — age × consent × activity landscape matrix. NOT a canonical artefact. See README.md for the experiment's purpose, the 80% confidence rule, and the '?' convention.

jurisdictions.label: United States (federal + CA AADC + state social-media laws)
Germany (EU MS, 16-floor default)
jurisdictions.consent_age_source: GDPR Art 8 default; DE did not derogate (verified, gdpr-text.com + EDPB tracker — secondary for DE-specific 16)

jurisdictions.label: France (EU MS, 15-floor)
jurisdictions.consent_age_source: GDPR Art 8; FR derogated to 15 (EDPB tracker — secondary)

jurisdictions.label: Slovakia (EU MS, 16-floor or 15 — see confidence)
jurisdictions.consent_age_source: GDPR Art 8; SK did not derogate (EDPB tracker — secondary; synthesis flagged PT/SK as unverified)

jurisdictions.label: Sweden (EU MS, 13-floor)
jurisdictions.consent_age_source: GDPR Art 8; SE derogated to 13 (EDPB tracker — secondary)

jurisdictions.label: Denmark (EU MS, 13-floor)
jurisdictions.consent_age_source: GDPR Art 8; DK derogated to 13 (EDPB tracker — secondary)

jurisdictions.label: Estonia (EU MS, 13 or 16 — see confidence)
jurisdictions.consent_age_source: GDPR Art 8; EE position on derogation unclear in our corpus

jurisdictions.label: Greece (EU MS, 15-floor)
jurisdictions.consent_age_source: GDPR Art 8; GR derogated to 15 (EDPB tracker — secondary)

activities.label: General data processing (storage, retrieval, internal use)
LLM-conversation content (output filtering, memory, routing, crisis handling)
AI-specific behavioural prohibitions (vulnerability exploitation, emotion inference, transparency)
Account-existence posture (Family Sharing / Google Family Link)

knowledge_states.label: Controller has no actual knowledge that user is below the consent age
Controller has actual knowledge that user is below the consent age (COPPA actual-knowledge trigger; GDPR/UK/NO 'reasonable efforts' activated)

cells.controlling_rule: COPPA applies only if (a) service is 'directed to children' OR (b) controller has actual knowledge user is under 13. If neither, no COPPA jurisdiction; FTC §5 still binds unfair/deceptive practices.
cells.consent_unlockable_note: VPC unlocks processing for under-13s
cells.confidence_rationale: Direct from synthesis §2 Layer 1 + verified 16 CFR Part 312 source.

cells.controlling_rule: COPPA binds. Verifiable Parental Consent (VPC) required before any personal-info collection. The 'actual knowledge' trigger covers DOB, grade-level, school type, or in-app chat where a child says 'I'm 10'. 2025 Rule amendments add retention cap + separate VPC for sale/targeted advertising + biometric / government-ID identifiers added to 'personal information' definition.
cells.consent_unlockable_note: VPC unlocks; FTC-approved methods enumerated (credit card, government-ID match, knowledge-based, video conference, etc.)
cells.confidence_rationale: Direct from synthesis §2 Layer 1 + §2 Layer 5 + verified 16 CFR Part 312 + verified Wikipedia COPPA summary. 2025 Rule text itself is unverified (FTC site 403'd) — flagged.

cells.controlling_rule: No COPPA jurisdiction. CAN-SPAM Act and state consumer-protection laws apply (not child-specific). California AADC (AB-2273) residual obligations post-NetChoice v. Bonta may apply to services likely accessed by children; what remains enforceable post-NetChoice needs counsel review.
cells.consent_unlockable_note: Generally yes; AADC carve-outs TBD by counsel
cells.confidence_rationale: Synthesis flags AADC post-NetChoice as unverified; CAN-SPAM not researched in this corpus.

cells.controlling_rule: COPPA binds. 2025 Rule amendments: separate VPC required for sale or targeted advertising of children's personal information. Restriction on disclosure of children's data to third parties for targeted advertising / behavioural profiling.
cells.consent_unlockable_note: Separate VPC required for sale/targeted ads even where general VPC exists
cells.confidence_rationale: Synthesis §2 Layer 2 cites 2025 Rule amendments; the actual text of the new sale/advertising rule is unverified (FTC 403'd).

cells.controlling_rule: No COPPA jurisdiction. FTC §5 unfair/deceptive practices may apply if profiling produces harmful outcomes; no child-specific profiling statute at federal level in our corpus.
cells.consent_unlockable_note: Generally yes; FTC §5 case-by-case
cells.confidence_rationale: Derivative from synthesis's general FTC §5 framing; no child-specific profiling statute surfaced.

cells.controlling_rule: COPPA's 2025 amendments restrict disclosure of children's data to third parties for behavioural profiling. FTC §5 still applies. AI Act does not apply (US jurisdiction). State-level laws (e.g. CA AADC residual) may add profiling restrictions — counsel to confirm scope post-NetChoice.
cells.consent_unlockable_note: Behavioural profiling restricted even with VPC; specific scope per 2025 Rule text (unverified)
cells.confidence_rationale: 2025 Rule text on behavioural profiling is unverified (FTC 403'd); CA AADC scope post-NetChoice unverified.

cells.controlling_rule: No COPPA jurisdiction. General FTC §5 + state consumer-protection laws apply to third-party disclosure.
cells.consent_unlockable_note: Generally yes; standard data-broker / privacy-law framework
cells.confidence_rationale: Derivative; no specific child-relevant third-party disclosure rule surfaced in our corpus outside COPPA.

cells.controlling_rule: COPPA binds. 2025 Rule amendments: separate VPC required before sale of children's personal information; restriction on disclosure to third parties for targeted advertising / behavioural profiling. Operates on the data, not just the consent.
cells.consent_unlockable_note: Separate VPC required for sale even where general VPC exists
cells.confidence_rationale: 2025 Rule text on sale/targeted-ad is unverified; secondary source (IAPP) is the basis.

cells.controlling_rule: No COPPA jurisdiction. OpenAI Model Spec Root-level U18 protections apply (platform terms, global). Gemini under-18 audience prohibition does NOT bind (no actual knowledge). Anthropic CSAM-reporting commitment applies (platform terms, global). FTC 6(b) inquiry (Sept 2025) signals that crisis handling should be structural, not improvised.
cells.consent_unlockable_note: Platform terms apply regardless of consent; FTC §5 crisis-handling posture applies if user is actually a child
cells.confidence_rationale: Platform terms verified (OpenAI Model Spec URL verified, Gemini terms verified, Anthropic AUP verified). FTC 6(b) order text unverified (FTC 403'd).

cells.controlling_rule: COPPA binds (VPC required before personal-info collection, which includes conversation logs with PII). OpenAI Model Spec Root-level U18 protections apply. **Gemini under-18 audience prohibition binds — Terms-of-Service breach** (Gap A in synthesis). Anthropic CSAM-reporting commitment applies. FTC 6(b) crisis-handling signal applies. Crisis-escalation is prompt-only (Gap C in synthesis) — no structural escalation channel, no guardian notification, no human-in-loop hold.
cells.consent_unlockable_note: VPC unlocks data processing but does NOT unlock Gemini routing (Gap A — Terms breach independent of consent); does NOT unlock prompt-only crisis handling being sufficient (Gap C)
cells.confidence_rationale: Multi-source: synthesis §4 Gaps A and C + verified Gemini terms + verified OpenAI Model Spec. The strongest populated cell in the matrix.

cells.controlling_rule: EU AI Act does not apply (US jurisdiction). OpenAI Model Spec Root-level U18 protections apply. Gemini under-18 audience prohibition does NOT bind (no actual knowledge). Anthropic CSAM-reporting commitment applies. FTC 6(b) inquiry (Sept 2025) on AI-chatbot operators is a US-side signal; no specific standard yet on age-vulnerability or emotion-inference.
cells.consent_unlockable_note: Platform terms apply regardless
cells.confidence_rationale: Platform terms verified; FTC 6(b) order text unverified (FTC 403'd).

cells.controlling_rule: EU AI Act does not apply. **OpenAI Model Spec Root-level U18 protections bind** (dev system prompts cannot lower them — verified in synthesis). **Gemini under-18 audience prohibition binds** (verified Terms). Anthropic CSAM-reporting binds. COPPA binds the underlying personal-info collection. FTC 6(b) crisis-handling signal applies.
cells.consent_unlockable_note: VPC does NOT unlock OpenAI Root-level U18 protections; does NOT unlock Gemini under-18 prohibition; does NOT unlock Anthropic CSAM-reporting
cells.confidence_rationale: Direct from synthesis §2 Layer 3 + verified OpenAI Model Spec URL + verified Gemini Terms URL + verified Anthropic AUP URL.

cells.controlling_rule: No COPPA jurisdiction. Standard FTC §5 + state consumer-protection retention norms apply. COPPA 2025 retention cap does not apply to unknown-age users.
cells.consent_unlockable_note: Generally yes; standard data-retention practice
cells.confidence_rationale: Derivative; specific 2025 Rule retention cap text is unverified.

cells.controlling_rule: COPPA binds. **2025 Rule amendments require a written retention policy, retention 'no longer than reasonably necessary,' and restrict disclosure of children's data to third parties for targeted advertising / behavioural profiling.** Retention values are deferred to counsel per data-model.md §4.9 (the `person_retain` seam).
cells.consent_unlockable_note: VPC unlocks collection but does not unlock unlimited retention; specific TTL values per 2025 Rule text (unverified)
cells.confidence_rationale: Synthesis §4 Gap D cites the 2025 retention cap; the actual cap text is unverified (FTC 403'd). Data-model.md §4.9 verified in-repo.

cells.controlling_rule: COPPA's actual-knowledge doctrine: account existence alone (Family Sharing / Google Family Link) is NOT a shield — but it is not a trigger either, in the unknown-knowledge state. Standard COPPA 'directed to children' / 'actual knowledge' analysis applies.
cells.consent_unlockable_note: Account existence does not unlock or trigger — it's a fact-of-the-world variable, not a rule
cells.confidence_rationale: Synthesis §2 Layer 5 + verified 16 CFR Part 312 actual-knowledge Rule text.

cells.controlling_rule: COPPA's actual-knowledge doctrine has been triggered. **The '3b posture' (rebuttable presumption from account existence) is 'unclear, not no' in the US.** Best-available argument: design the app so no age information is ever collected at the controller layer (no DOB field, no grade-level capture, no in-app chat that surfaces age signals), treat the Family Sharing / Family Link account as a signal of parental gate, and rely on the actual-knowledge doctrine's ignorance defense. This is a posture, not a safe harbor. Counsel must rule.
cells.consent_unlockable_note: Arguable: VPC can be substituted by 'engineered ignorance' — but the shield evaporates the moment a child types 'I'm 10' in chat
cells.confidence_rationale: Synthesis §2 Layer 5 'Headline arguable finding' (US 3b) — the most genuinely arguable posture in the corpus. Specific named cases (Wildec, TikTok) cited but unverified primary text.

cells.controlling_rule: UK GDPR Art 8 (retained post-Brexit) + DPA 2018 §123 applies. Threshold 13. Above 13, child self-consents. Children's Code 'likely accessed by children' applies to under-18 services regardless of actual user age.
cells.consent_unlockable_note: Self-consent unlocks for 13+; below 13 needs parental consent
cells.confidence_rationale: Synthesis §2 Layer 1 + verified DPA 2018 §123 + verified gdpr-text.com Art 8. ICO landing page 403'd for direct verification of threshold text.

cells.controlling_rule: UK GDPR Art 8 binds. Below 13, parental consent required. Controller must make 'reasonable efforts' to verify. Children's Code 'best interests' duty applies. The 'actual knowledge' trigger is broader than COPPA — 'likely to be accessed by children' is a design obligation that applies regardless of controller knowledge of specific user. ICO v. TikTok (Apr 2023, £12.7M) is the headline enforcement precedent.
cells.consent_unlockable_note: Parental consent unlocks data processing, but does not unlock all Children's Code standards (e.g. profiling off-by-default, no nudge techniques)
cells.confidence_rationale: Synthesis §2 Layer 1 + Layer 2. ICO TikTok fine amount cited from secondary; ICO primary text 403'd.

cells.controlling_rule: Children's Code applies to services 'likely to be accessed by children' — broader than COPPA. Marketing to under-18s restricted by Children's Code standards. No 'actual knowledge' gate — likely-accessed-by-children is the trigger.
cells.consent_unlockable_note: Children's Code applies to under-18 regardless of consent posture; consent doesn't unlock the marketing standards
cells.confidence_rationale: Synthesis §2 Layer 2 references Children's Code marketing standards; specific standards text unverified (ICO 403'd).

cells.controlling_rule: Children's Code applies. ICO has issued enforcement notices against Snap, BeReal, etc. (2023–2024) for marketing-to-minors failures. Standards include: data minimisation, no detrimental use of children's data, no nudge techniques.
cells.consent_unlockable_note: Consent (parental or self) does not unlock the marketing-to-minors restrictions
cells.confidence_rationale: ICO enforcement actions cited; specific Children's Code marketing-standards text unverified.

cells.controlling_rule: Children's Code Standard on profiling: profiling off-by-default for under-18s. Applies to 'likely accessed by children' services regardless of specific user.
cells.consent_unlockable_note: Profiling off-by-default for under-18; consent cannot unlock by default
cells.confidence_rationale: Synthesis §2 Layer 6 references 'profiling off by default'; specific Code Standard text unverified.

cells.controlling_rule: Children's Code profiling-off-by-default applies. AI Act high-risk classification (Annex III §3) applies if product evaluates learning outcomes — irrespective of age, see Layer 1 / Layer 6 cross-reference.
cells.consent_unlockable_note: Profiling restrictions apply regardless of consent for under-18; AI Act Annex III obligations apply if product is high-risk-classified
cells.confidence_rationale: Combined UK Children's Code + EU AI Act; both verified in part.

cells.controlling_rule: Children's Code data minimisation + no detrimental use standards apply to third-party disclosure for under-18s.
cells.consent_unlockable_note: Children's Code applies; specific third-party-disclosure Standard text unverified
cells.confidence_rationale: Derivative from Children's Code general standards; specific third-party Standard unverified.

cells.controlling_rule: Same as unknown — Children's Code applies to under-18s regardless. ICO enforcement has targeted third-party data flows to minors.
cells.consent_unlockable_note: Children's Code applies; specific restrictions TBD by counsel
cells.confidence_rationale: Derivative.

cells.controlling_rule: OpenAI Model Spec Root-level U18 protections apply (platform terms, global). Gemini under-18 audience prohibition does NOT bind (no actual knowledge). Anthropic CSAM-reporting applies.
cells.consent_unlockable_note: Platform terms apply regardless
cells.confidence_rationale: Platform terms verified.

cells.controlling_rule: Gemini under-18 audience prohibition binds (Terms-of-Service breach — Gap A). OpenAI Root-level U18 protections apply. Anthropic CSAM-reporting applies. Children's Code 'best interests of the child' standard applies to under-18 conversations. Crisis-escalation is prompt-only (Gap C) — no structural escalation channel.
cells.consent_unlockable_note: Parental consent does not unlock Gemini routing, OpenAI Root protections, or structural crisis handling
cells.confidence_rationale: Multi-source: platform terms verified + synthesis §4 Gap C + Children's Code best-interests.

cells.controlling_rule: EU AI Act does not directly apply post-Brexit, but the UK is developing its own AI regulatory approach (pro-innovation, sector-by-sector). Children's Code best-interests standard applies. Platform terms (OpenAI / Anthropic / Gemini) apply globally.
cells.consent_unlockable_note: UK AI regulation is in flux; Children's Code best-interests applies
cells.confidence_rationale: UK post-Brexit AI regulatory framework not researched in this corpus beyond general framing.

cells.controlling_rule: OpenAI Root-level U18 binds. Gemini under-18 audience prohibition binds. Anthropic CSAM-reporting binds. Children's Code best-interests applies. No UK AI Act equivalent; relies on platform terms + general Children's Code standards.
cells.consent_unlockable_note: Consent does not unlock OpenAI Root, Gemini, or Anthropic
cells.confidence_rationale: Platform terms verified.

cells.controlling_rule: UK GDPR Article 5(1)(e) storage limitation applies. Children's Code data minimisation standard applies. No child-specific retention statute in UK beyond general UK GDPR.
cells.consent_unlockable_note: Children's Code data minimisation applies to under-18
cells.confidence_rationale: Derivative; specific retention-rule text unverified.

cells.controlling_rule: UK GDPR Article 5(1)(e) + Children's Code data minimisation apply. ICO enforcement has targeted data-retention failures for under-18s.
cells.consent_unlockable_note: Children's Code data minimisation applies
cells.confidence_rationale: Derivative.

cells.controlling_rule: No ICO statement that Family Sharing / Google Family Link functions as a consent signal. Children's Code applies to 'likely accessed by children' services regardless of account type.
cells.consent_unlockable_note: Account existence does not unlock Children's Code obligations
cells.confidence_rationale: Synthesis §2 Layer 2: 'No ICO statement that Family Sharing or Google Family Link functions as a consent signal.'

cells.controlling_rule: Best-interests duty bites the moment operator has any signal that user is a child. No platform-side consent signal blessed by ICO. 'Reasonable efforts' to verify (UK GDPR Art 8(2) equivalent) required; EDPB-style analysis applies by analogy.
cells.consent_unlockable_note: Account existence does not shield — best-interests duty binds regardless
cells.confidence_rationale: Synthesis §2 Layer 2 + Layer 5.

cells.controlling_rule: GDPR Art 8 default 16-floor applies (DE did not derogate). Above 16, child self-consents. 14–16 parental consent required; below 14 parental consent required + 'reasonable efforts' to verify. Children's Code equivalent: BDSG (Bundesdatenschutzgesetz) has child-data provisions; specific rules not researched in this corpus.
cells.consent_unlockable_note: Self-consent at 16+; below 16 needs parental consent
cells.confidence_rationale: Verified GDPR Art 8 + EDPB tracker (secondary).

cells.controlling_rule: GDPR Art 8 binds. Below 16, parental consent required. Controller must make 'reasonable efforts' to verify (Art 8(2)). EDPB Guidelines 05/2020 §3 (paragraph-level text unverified by us) — mechanisms relying solely on the child or a platform are insufficient. AI Act Art 5(1)(b) age-vulnerability exploitation prohibition also binds (irrespective of consent — see ai_behavioural cell).
cells.consent_unlockable_note: Parental consent unlocks data processing; does not unlock AI Act Art 5(1)(b) prohibitions
cells.confidence_rationale: GDPR Art 8 verified; EDPB §3 paragraph text unverified (EDPB PDF body not directly fetched).

cells.controlling_rule: GDPR Recital 38 specific protection for children re: marketing. UWG (Gesetz gegen den unlauteren Wettbewerb) has marketing-to-minors restrictions. AI Act Art 5(1)(b) age-vulnerability exploitation prohibition (live legal-uncertainty surface — no Commission guidance yet) covers AI-driven marketing to children.
cells.consent_unlockable_note: GDPR Recital 38 applies; UWG specific rules not researched; AI Act Art 5(1)(b) binds regardless of consent
cells.confidence_rationale: GDPR Recital 38 verified; UWG not researched; AI Act Art 5(1)(b) guidance pending.

cells.controlling_rule: Same as unknown + stricter interpretation under UWG and AI Act Art 5(1)(b) when controller has actual knowledge. Markedsføringsloven-style marketing-to-minors restrictions vary by EU MS; German UWG specifics not researched.
cells.consent_unlockable_note: AI Act Art 5(1)(b) binds regardless; UWG specifics TBD
cells.confidence_rationale: AI Act Art 5(1)(b) verified; UWG specifics not in corpus.

cells.controlling_rule: GDPR Art 22 + Recital 71 prohibit solely-automated decisions with legal/significant effects on children. AI Act Annex III §3 high-risk classification for AI in education (evaluates learning outcomes) applies if product is high-risk — irrespective of age, per synthesis §2 Layer 1.
cells.consent_unlockable_note: GDPR Art 22 restrictions apply regardless of consent; AI Act Annex III obligations apply to high-risk AI systems
cells.confidence_rationale: GDPR Art 22 + AI Act Annex III verified.

cells.controlling_rule: Same as unknown + AI Act Art 5(1)(b) age-vulnerability exploitation prohibition binds (irrespective of consent). Per synthesis §2 Layer 6: 'a prompt that adopted an emotionally-bonding persona for a sub-13 user is the kind of thing Art 5(1)(b) reaches' (note: synthesis says 'sub-13' but the prohibition applies to all vulnerable ages including 13–17).
cells.consent_unlockable_note: Consent does not unlock AI Act Art 5(1)(b) or Art 22
cells.confidence_rationale: AI Act Art 5(1)(b) verified; vulnerability scope for 13–17 is synthesis-derived.

cells.controlling_rule: GDPR Art 6/9 lawful basis required for third-party disclosure. Children's data does not have a separate third-party-disclosure rule at EU level beyond general GDPR.
cells.consent_unlockable_note: Standard GDPR lawful basis applies
cells.confidence_rationale: Derivative; no child-specific EU third-party-disclosure rule surfaced.

cells.controlling_rule: Same as unknown + AI Act Annex III deployer notification obligation (Art 26) if product is high-risk. AI Act Art 5(1)(b) binds for age-vulnerability exploitation via third-party AI integrations.
cells.consent_unlockable_note: AI Act Art 5(1)(b) binds regardless; Annex III deployer notification if high-risk
cells.confidence_rationale: AI Act Art 5(1)(b) and Annex III verified.

cells.controlling_rule: Platform terms apply globally (OpenAI Root, Gemini under-18 audience — does not bind without actual knowledge, Anthropic CSAM). GDPR Recital 38 specific protection for children re: marketing/profiling within conversations. AI Act Art 50 transparency to user (you are talking to an AI) — 'obvious from context' carve-out; whether a one-on-one tutor persona qualifies is unsettled (synthesis §2 Layer 6).
cells.consent_unlockable_note: AI Act Art 50 binds regardless of consent (transparency obligation); 'obvious from context' carve-out TBD
cells.confidence_rationale: AI Act Art 50 verified; 'obvious from context' interpretation is open.

cells.controlling_rule: Platform terms bind: OpenAI Root-level U18 (verified), Gemini under-18 audience prohibition (verified Terms), Anthropic CSAM-reporting (verified). AI Act Art 50 transparency obligation binds. AI Act Art 5(1)(b) age-vulnerability exploitation prohibition binds. AI Act Art 5(1)(f) emotion-inference prohibition in 'educational institutions' — scope of 'educational institutions' is unsettled (synthesis §2 Layer 6). Crisis-escalation is prompt-only (Gap C); AI Act and platform terms arguably require structural routing.
cells.consent_unlockable_note: Consent (parental or self) does not unlock AI Act Art 5(1)(b), Art 5(1)(f), Art 50, OpenAI Root, Gemini under-18 prohibition, or Anthropic CSAM-reporting
cells.confidence_rationale: AI Act Art 5(1)(b), (f), Art 50 all verified; platform terms verified; Gap C from synthesis §4.

cells.controlling_rule: AI Act Art 5(1)(b) age-vulnerability exploitation prohibition applies (live legal-uncertainty — no Commission guidance yet on what counts as 'exploitation due to age' for an LLM-tutor). AI Act Art 5(1)(f) emotion-inference prohibition in 'educational institutions' — scope unsettled. AI Act Art 50 transparency to user (with 'obvious from context' carve-out).
cells.consent_unlockable_note: AI Act Art 5 prohibitions bind regardless of consent
cells.confidence_rationale: AI Act Art 5 verified; live legal-uncertainty flagged in synthesis §2 Layer 6.

cells.controlling_rule: AI Act Art 5(1)(b) binds — applies to all vulnerable ages, not just under-13. AI Act Art 5(1)(f) binds in 'educational institutions' (scope unsettled). AI Act Art 50 binds. OpenAI Model Spec Root-level U18 protections apply (verified). Gemini under-18 audience prohibition binds. Anthropic CSAM-reporting applies. The strongest non-statutory binding: the prohibition on age-vulnerability exploitation cannot be unlocked by any consent posture.
cells.consent_unlockable_note: AI Act Art 5 prohibitions and platform terms bind regardless
cells.confidence_rationale: AI Act Art 5(1)(b) verified; platform terms verified.

cells.controlling_rule: GDPR Art 5(1)(e) storage limitation applies. No EU-level child-specific retention statute beyond general GDPR.
cells.consent_unlockable_note: Standard GDPR lawful basis applies
cells.confidence_rationale: Derivative; no child-specific EU retention rule surfaced.

cells.controlling_rule: Same as unknown. AI Act high-risk classification (Annex III §3) imposes record-keeping requirements if product is high-risk — irrespective of age.
cells.consent_unlockable_note: AI Act record-keeping if high-risk
cells.confidence_rationale: AI Act Annex III verified.

cells.controlling_rule: GDPR Art 8(2) 'reasonable efforts to verify' applies. EDPB Guidelines 05/2020 §3 (paragraph text unverified by us) — mechanisms relying solely on the child or a platform (e.g. a parent holding an Apple ID) are insufficient. No platform-side consent signal blessed by EDPB or German DPA.
cells.consent_unlockable_note: Account existence does not satisfy 'reasonable efforts' per EDPB
cells.confidence_rationale: EDPB §3 paragraph text unverified; general 'reasonable efforts' framing verified.

cells.controlling_rule: GDPR Art 8(2) 'reasonable efforts' triggered. Account existence does not shield. Irish DPC TikTok fine (2024, €345M, amount unverified by us) found platform-side mechanisms (e.g. 'Family Pairing') insufficient. German DPA would likely follow Irish DPC reasoning.
cells.consent_unlockable_note: Account existence does not satisfy 'reasonable efforts'
cells.confidence_rationale: EDPB + Irish DPC reasoning; €345M figure unverified (DPC press release not directly fetched).
Not populated in this proof-of-concept pass. See SOURCES.md for Norway's personopplysningsloven §5 (verified) and Markedsføringsloven (verified); the per-activity cell mapping is future work.
Not populated in this proof-of-concept pass. France's 15-floor is verified via EDPB tracker (secondary).
Not populated in this proof-of-concept pass. Synthesis flagged PT/SK as unverified in the EDPB Member-State list.
13-floor verified via EDPB tracker (secondary). Per-activity cell mapping is future work.
13-floor verified via EDPB tracker (secondary). Per-activity cell mapping is future work.

Not populated in this proof-of-concept pass. Estonia's consent-age derogation under GDPR Art 8 is unclear in our corpus. Estonia is a useful case for EU AI Act regulatory sandbox activity; not researched in this pass.
15-floor verified via EDPB tracker (secondary). Per-activity cell mapping is future work.
